title: Smoky Velvety Irish Pork Stew
description: A hearty, smoky Irish stew featuring tender pork, sweet apples, and a hint of bhuna spice, perfect for a cozy sick day. This dish is gluten-free-friendly, making it accessible to those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Brown the pork cubes, then set aside.
Add more oil if needed, then sauté the chopped onions until they're translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, bhuna spice mix, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped apples, browned pork, chicken broth, and cubed potatoes. Stir to combine, then cover the Dutch oven.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and braise for 2.5 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and stir in the heavy cream.
Return the Dutch oven to the stovetop and simmer over low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
